  {
    "id": "ddx-06b77652",
    "title": "ADR-style: 7 reliability principles for ddx try / ddx work / sub-agent execution",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-03T19:55:41.651384066Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-05T03:08:28.336806239Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "area:server",
      "kind:design",
      "reliability",
      "bead-quality"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.\n\nThe 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.\n\nPRINCIPLE STATEMENTS\n\nP1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.\n\nP2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.\n\nP3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see \"preflight skipped (no operator pin)\" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.\n\nP4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.\n\nP5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.\n\nP6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).\n\nP7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)\n- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces\n- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads\n\nEVIDENCE\n- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.",
    "acceptance": "1. docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md exists and contains all 7 principles with their statements above (verbatim).\n2. README sections of each refactor child (C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C9, C11, C12, C13) cite the principle(s) the child enforces. Cross-link in those bead descriptions added via ddx bead update.\n3. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md updated with one-line pointers: 'See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.'\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... still green (no code change; sanity check that nothing broke).\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\n6. Conventional commit ending [\u003cthis-bead-id\u003e].",
    "notes": "Evidence cross-link: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and serves as evidence for P7 / bead-quality."
  },

    "description": "PROBLEM\n  The drain loop tracks attempted and hookFailed beads via ad-hoc in-memory maps rather than a structured Guard contract, making the loop body untestable in isolation and the skip policy implicit.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:403-413 declares attempted := make(map[string]struct{}) and hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{}). Lines 534-544 manually clear both maps between sleep cycles. Lines 591-601 implement the two-strikes pre-claim hook policy by mutating these maps inline. nextCandidate (line 1216) accepts attempted as a parameter and checks membership at line 1223. No Guard interface exists; the pre-claim logic and the complexity gate each embed their skip logic directly in the loop body.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Define Guard interface in cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go: Allow(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (bool, reason string).\n  - Implement two concrete Guards: PreClaimGuard (wraps runtime.PreClaimHook two-strikes logic) and ComplexityGuard (wraps w.ComplexityGate nil-check).\n  - Delete attempted and hookFailed maps from execute_bead_loop.go:403-413. Replace nextCandidate's attempted parameter with drain.result.Results slice (already populated per-iteration): filter beads whose ID appears in Results.\n  - Pre-claim hook denial: call w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, 30s, ...) instead of recording in hookFailed.\n  - Add TestGuard_PreClaim_TwoStrikesSkips and TestGuard_Complexity_NilGateWarnsOnce to a new cli/internal/agent/work/guard_test.go.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - Heartbeat extraction (C11, ddx-0b8780db).\n  - File renames (C13, ddx-387a0178).\n  - Any change to SetExecutionCooldown duration policy (controlled by caller constants).",

Pre-claim hook denial calls w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown with 30s cooldown; hookFailed logic removed.\n6. The ownerSpoke routing metadata fields (nodeID, spokeURL) are populated by existing fan-out code but no write-routing contract enforces \"hub forwards, never broadcasts.\"\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Add write-routing helper in cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_federation.go: routeToOwner(ctx, ownerSpokeURL, mutationQuery, variables) error that forwards mutations via HTTP to the owning spoke.\n  - Wire routeToOwner into the relevant mutation resolvers in resolver_mutation_beads.go or resolver_mutation_runs.go for any mutation that operates on a federated resource.\n  - Add schema.graphql comment block documenting the write-routing contract (\"hub forwards to ownerSpoke; never broadcasts\").\n  - Name the ownerSpoke field on FederatedBead/FederatedRun/FederatedProject as the routing discriminator.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - New non-federated resolvers (covered by Story 13).\n  - Spoke discovery and registration (covered by ddx-fe63969f).\n  - Federated mutations on FederatedProject (out of Story 14 scope; read-only for projects).",

  {

    "parent": "ddx-5cb6e6cd",
    "description": "PROBLEM\n  The heartbeat goroutine in the drain loop is inlined, coupling ticker lifecycle management with candidate execution and blocking unit tests from verifying heartbeat behavior without a real ticker.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:732-753 inlines a goroutine + sync.WaitGroup + time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval) directly in the loop body. hbCtx and hbWG are declared locally; the goroutine calls w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID) on every tick. Drain loop caller of withHeartbeat is the production site (execute_bead_loop.go or its successor after C13 rename).\n4. It does not yet forbid direct field assignment of Profile/Harness/Model on Runtime structs inside run/try/work command code paths. The SD-024 runtimelint analyzer (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint/analyzer.go:41) forbids durable-knob fields on *Runtime structs generically, but does not name a per-command allowlist or produce a test that verifies the run/work/try paths specifically. No TestRoutingLint* test exercises this invariant.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - Extend routinglint (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer.go) to flag direct Profile/Harness/Model field assignment on types matching *Runtime in cli/cmd/ run/work/try command files, or add a new rule in runtimelint covering this.\n  - Alternatively, add a targeted integration test TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly that runs the analyzer against the live tree and asserts zero diagnostics for the assignment pattern.\n  - Fix any existing violations: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1893-1897 passes overrides.Profile/Harness/Model through CLIOverrides to config.LoadAndResolve — verify this is compliant (CLIOverrides is not a Runtime struct).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - Changes to agent_run or agent_run_overrides command behavior.\n  - Changes to the fizeau or ladder routing logic.\n  - Lint rules for non-routing fields on Runtime structs (covered by runtimelint).",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/tools/lint/routinglint/ (package routinglint) contains the new rule or an integration test that enforces the Profile/Harness/Model-on-Runtime prohibition for run/try/work paths.\n2. TestViolations (cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer_test.go:12) still passes.\n4.     "id": "ddx-1e516bc9",
    "title": "fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models (upstream gap)",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-05-03T01:06:53.276389609Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-03T01:17:15.675058816Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "kind:fix",
      "upstream-fizeau",
      "observed-failure"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "Upstream fizeau gap: when ServiceExecuteRequest carries an explicit Harness pin AND empty Model AND a non-empty Profile/MinPower, fizeau v0.10.3's Execute (service_execute.go:193-224) treats Harness as a hard pin and skips the routing engine entirely (it only invokes routing when req.Harness == \"\"). Result: request validates with empty Model, fizeau returns Model=\"\", DDx passes no --model arg to the claude CLI, and Anthropic's CLI uses whatever the local user's session default is.\n\nOBSERVED\n- Active worker on ddx-23c67ca4 (PID 4065353): claude --print -p --verbose ... — no --model arg\n- ddx server workers show worker-20260503T004125-0b67 → Model: empty, Harness: claude\n- Operators cannot control which Claude model executes a bead via DDx routing for the claude harness; UI/spec.json reports Model=\"\" (operator sees \"claude\" but not which Claude model)\n\nSCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY\n\nThis bead is HALF A of the original ddx-1e516bc9 problem. HALF B (DDx supplying Profile/MinPower in ServiceExecuteRequest) is owned by ddx-cfedee8e + C9 (ddx-9228a484), which wire the ladder into the executor closure and bump MinPower per each call. Without the ladder wiring, fizeau wouldn't have anything to route on even if this fix lands. With the ladder wiring AND this fizeau fix, the full path works.\n\nINVESTIGATION FIRST\nConfirm the gap before implementing. The hypothesis is that fizeau v0.10.3 service_execute.go:193-224 only runs routing when Harness == \"\". If that's wrong (e.g., fizeau already routes within harness when Profile/MinPower is supplied), this bead is a no-op and gets closed as already-resolved.\n\nUPSTREAM CHANGE (if confirmed)\nModify fizeau's Execute so that when Harness is set and Model is empty and Profile or MinPower is non-empty, the routing engine runs WITHIN that harness's eligible models. Fizeau already has the catalog (models.yaml: opus-4.7 code-high :31-60; sonnet-4.6 code-medium :141-170; claude-haiku-5.5 code-economy :222-251) and harness eligibility (registry.go: claude/codex AutoRoutingEligible=true; gemini/opencode/pi false). The change is: skip the \"Harness != '' means skip routing\" gate when Model is empty AND a routing input (Profile/MinPower) is present.\n\nFor Class 2 harnesses (gemini/opencode/pi, AutoRoutingEligible=false): the engine should still surface a clear \"no auto-resolution available\" error — NOT silently return empty Model. DDx should treat that as a fail-fast on the attempt.\n\nDDx-SIDE WORK (verification only — not implementation)\nOnce the ladder is wired (cfedee8e → C9), DDx will already submit Profile/MinPower for each Execute call. This bead's verification phase asserts that with cfedee8e landed AND fizeau bumped to the version with this fix, ddx work --harness=claude produces a non-empty resolved model in spec.json / result.json / server worker UI.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- DDx-side ladder wiring or MinPower-supply changes (owned by ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-9228a484 C9)\n- Operator-explicit --model passes (already work; pinned by operator intent)\n- HTTP providers openrouter/lmstudio (no subprocess --model arg; not subprocess invocations)\n- Stale resolveModel() in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:380 (legacy path; codex confirmed production uses RunWithConfigViaService → svc.Execute, not the legacy Run() path)\n\nWHY P1 (was P0)\nDemoted because:\n- The DDx-side enabling work (cfedee8e/C9) is already P1/P2 in the queue\n- This bead's effect is only observable after cfedee8e/C9 land\n- It's an upstream fizeau bump, not a DDx code change\n- Original P0 framing assumed a DDx-side fix in the dispatch path; that part is the refactor's job\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-cfedee8e (or its absorber ddx-9228a484 C9): the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on",
    "acceptance": "1. Investigation phase. Read fizeau v0.10.3 source (cli/go.mod pin) and confirm whether explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower actually skips routing OR runs routing within the harness. Evidence committed at .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/fizeau-execute-routing-investigation.md with file:line citations into fizeau's source.     "id": "ddx-256af8b5",
    "title": "S15-7: Multi-node trust attestation forwarding (Story-14 coupling)",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T12:14:38.513016892Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T21:57:43.276940956Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "story:15",
      "kind:multi-node",
      "blocking",
      "triage:needs-investigation"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-1d85c927",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nBLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement. In the coordinator topology (Story 14), the originating peer identity attestation is not forwarded from coordinator to client — any non-localhost write goes through the hub without carrying who initiated it. The spoke cannot authorize on both origin and coordinator identity because neither is transmitted.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: current federation supports query fan-out only. No RouteMutationToProjectOwner, no ForwardMutation, no identity header propagation.\n- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.\n- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.\n\nPLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):\n- Multi-node routing (point 6): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations.\n- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.\n- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.\n\nThis bead has been decomposed into 4 children:\n- ddx-468d800f — S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers.\n- ddx-eb75a32d — S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation).\n- ddx-ba31fec8 — S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization.\n- ddx-ea1ada47 — S15-7d: Integration test with two in-process servers.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- New FEAT spec; changes go into FEAT-010 and FEAT-011 extensions.\n- Performance optimization of federation routing.\n- Story 14 query fan-out behavior (already shipped in fanout.go).",
    "acceptance": "1. Coordinator forwards origin identity (X-DDx-Origin-Identity) unchanged in mutation envelope from ddx-eb75a32d.\n2. Client-side authorization (ddx-ba31fec8) checks both origin and coordinator identity; returns 403 when either missing or untrusted.\n3. ADR-conformance lint (ddx-468d800f) added to CI and fails on any new mutation resolver missing requireTrusted or CSRF/Origin checks.\n4. TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_AuthorizesOriginAndCoordinator passes (happy path).\n5. TestFederation_OperatorPromptSubmit_CoordinatorToSpoke_IdentityUnchanged (ddx-ea1ada47) exercises full stack.\n6. Three negative sub-tests (untrusted-coordinator, missing-origin, tampered-origin) all return 403.\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",
    "notes": "decomposed into ddx-468d800f (ADR-conformance lint), ddx-eb75a32d (mutation routing envelope), ddx-ba31fec8 (origin identity forwarding + dual auth), ddx-ea1ada47 (integration test). Original 4 ACs spanned federation infrastructure that does not yet exist (mutation routing — current federation only supports query fan-out), identity propagation, CI lint tooling, and an E2E test — too large for one execute-bead pass.",

    "description": "PROBLEM\nExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. This is the root cause of the five flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) being dropped on the server-managed-worker path, causing \"ddx work --harness claude\" to fail harness validation when routed through the server.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nEach execute-loop flag traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:\n\n1. cobra flag extract + local var: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 (fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once, pollInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, maxPower extracted as naked locals)\n\n2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 — these locals are closed over by singleTierAttempt and assembled into config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them\n\n3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition (worker-side canonical): cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 — typed, but hand-maintained parallel to cobra; missing MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev\n\n4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct (REST handler): cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 — SEPARATE anonymous struct; the five dropped flags and OpaquePassthrough are absent here, causing them to be silently lost for server-spawned workers\n\n5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 + cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 — hand-assembled from the request struct fields; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here\n\n6. GraphQL adapter request struct + StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 + 127-141 — a THIRD parallel declaration (OpaquePassthrough absent here too); fixes to the REST path leave GraphQL re-broken\n\nThe consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has TWO validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- cli/internal/agent/spec.go (new file): define ExecuteLoopSpec with ALL fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.\n- cli/internal/agent/spec.go: add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods. Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) — the single cobra→spec conversion.\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec call. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.\n- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 + 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD (with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero).\n- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go (new file): TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does NOT change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.\n- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) — same smell may exist; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.\n- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.\n- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.\n\nSEQUENCING\nddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 — server-spawn migration to exec ddx work):\nLANDS INDEPENDENTLY and BEFORE ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 §\"Sequencing relative to in-flight work\" (line 553) is explicit: \"[ddx-29058e2a] remains valuable as a worker-side struct unification (cobra → in-process flow) and should land independently.\" When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration — but by then, the unified ExecuteLoopSpec already lives in cli/internal/agent/spec.go, which is the correct durable home for it regardless of transport. ddx-eccc6efb does NOT block or supersede this bead; this bead does NOT block ddx-eccc6efb. Coordination note: if ddx-eccc6efb lands first, the scope of this bead narrows (the server.go and graphql_adapters.go layers are already deleted), so cherry-pick only the cobra + workers.go layers.",
    "acceptance": "1. ExecuteLoopSpec struct defined in cli/internal/agent/spec.go (or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go). Exported fields include AT MINIMUM: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Once, PollInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 NOT in this list is a bug.\n\n2. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is NOT persisted to spec.json.\n\n3. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).\n\n4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev all survive the REST round-trip.\n\n5. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.\n\n6. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct.\n\n7. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling (with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero). No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.\n\n8. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips BOTH validation gates — workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) AND the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. Test: TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.\n\n9. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go (or executeloop sub-package). Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal (catches omitempty bugs that silently drop non-default-false fields). A future added field automatically falls under coverage.\n\n10. Backward compatibility: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Specifically: files containing legacy fields (e.g., min_tier, max_tier from older specs) are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do NOT enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.\n\n11. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion \u003e server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. Test: TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400.\n\n12. Regression: --local path (runAgentExecuteLoopImpl inline path) is unchanged in behavior. Existing tests that cover the inline path pass without modification.\n\n13. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green.\n\n14. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

    "id": "ddx-31f745cd",
    "title": "study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-03T19:55:41.880567652Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-05T03:08:30.860761453Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "area:tooling",
      "kind:study",
      "reliability",
      "bead-quality"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:\nH1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.\nH2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.\n\nBoth are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.\n\nTHREE PATHS\n\nPath A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).\nPath B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.\nPath C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.\n\nINTERPRETATION\n- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).\n- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).\n- A == B \u003c\u003c C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.\n\nMETHOD\nPick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.\n\nMEASUREMENTS\nPer path per bead:\n- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)\n- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)\n- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)\n- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored\n- Cost: tokens, USD\n- Manual interventions\n- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list\n\nOUTPUT\n.ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:\n- Per-bead per-path table\n- Aggregate stats (median + p99)\n- Failure-mode distribution\n- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)\n- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape\n\nTIMING\nThis study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.\n\nNOT IN SCOPE\n- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)\n- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)\n- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)\n\nEVIDENCE\n- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.",
    "acceptance": "1. 5 fixture beads chosen and listed in .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/comparison-fixtures.md with: bead ID, base-rev, original closing commit, characterization (trivial/refactor/test/multi-file/docs).\n2. Path A run: each fixture's bead replayed via ddx try (or ddx work --once) on a branch from base-rev~1; outcomes captured.\n3. Path B run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with bead's auto-prompt verbatim; outcomes captured.\n4. Path C run: each fixture dispatched via Agent tool with operator hand-curated prompt; outcomes captured.\n5. Per-fixture, per-path measurements table with the 7 named metrics.\n6. Aggregate stats + failure-mode distribution.\n7. Hypothesis verdict section: H1 / H2 / both / inconclusive.\n8. Recommendations section: which path for which bead-shape.\n9. Report committed at .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md.\n10. Conventional commit ending [\u003cthis-bead-id\u003e].\n11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.\n\nDEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).",

    "parent": "ddx-97335425",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nThe server documents resolver rebuilds the full artifact graph on every page request, even when the underlying graph has not changed. On a 2000-artifact corpus, this produces visible latency on every load and makes concurrent requests redundantly expensive.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go (or equivalent resolver file): the documents resolver calls the full graph-build pipeline on every GraphQL request without checking if the graph revision has changed since the last build.\n- No normalized artifact cache (by graph revision) exists — every request starts cold.\n- The search path in the documents resolver also rebuilds the full artifact list on every search call.\n- ddx-5681cc57 (2k fixture) is a dep: cache design must be validated against the 2k scale fixture.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add a cache keyed by graph revision (mtime-based, as recommended in the perf epic): store normalized artifact list (lowercased title/path/id/mediaType) keyed by revision string.\n- Add search to documents resolver: filter the cached normalized list by search query.\n- Add invalidation on file change: detect mtime changes and invalidate cache entry.\n- Ensure concurrent requests don't double-build (single-flight pattern or sync.Once per revision).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Caching for resolvers other than documents.\n- CI performance gate (that is a later bead).",
    "acceptance": "1. Document cache keyed by graph revision exists; cold request builds once and subsequent requests with same revision use cache.\n2. Concurrent requests for the same graph revision do not double-build (single-flight or equivalent).\n3. documents resolver supports a search parameter; filtered results come from cache.\n4. Cache invalidated on file change (mtime-based).\n5. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_HitOnSameRevision verifies warm request does not rebuild.\n6. TestDocumentsResolver_Cache_MissOnRevisionChange verifies cache miss triggers rebuild.\n7. TestDocumentsResolver_Search_FiltersFromCache verifies search uses cached data.\n8. Cold + warm latency benchmarked via B7-C1 fixture (results committed to .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/perf-cache-results.md).\n9. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... green.\n10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

  {
    "id": "ddx-633b16eb",
    "title": "cli: 'ddx --version' should warn when installed binary is behind source tree",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "bug",
    "created_at": "2026-05-04T02:17:58.552222092Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T22:21:53.260037672Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:cli",
      "kind:fix",
      "observed-failure",
      "reliability",
      "bead-quality"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nAfter rebuilding ddx (make install), the installed binary at ~/.local/bin/ddx is correct. But subsequent commits land in the source tree, and the operator forgets to rebuild. New flags / subcommands appear in source but silently disappear from the operator's CLI surface. Today's session bit this twice: 'ddx work plan --limit' worked, then later returned 'unknown flag: --limit' — not because the feature was broken, but because the binary was stale relative to source.\n\nOBSERVED (2026-05-03 session)\n- Built + installed at commit c0f95380; 'ddx work plan --limit' worked.\n- ~10 commits later: 'ddx work plan --limit' returned 'unknown flag.'\n- Source still had the flag definition; binary was stale.\n- Operator wasted time investigating a non-existent regression before remembering to rebuild.\n\nThis compounds because:\n1. Source can drift hours-to-days ahead of the installed binary during active development.\n2. Cobra's 'unknown flag' / 'unknown command' errors give NO hint that the binary is stale.\n3. ddx work auto-spawns workers — those workers also use the stale binary, propagating the gap.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/cmd/version.go implements 'ddx version' and 'ddx --version'. It prints the build-time-injected version string (e.g., 'v0.6.2-alpha12-12-g86848d21') from ldflags but does NOT compare against the source tree's current HEAD when invoked from inside a ddx project repo. No stale-binary detection exists in cli/cmd/doctor.go either. The build SHA is injected at link time via main.go -ldflags but never compared against runtime git rev-parse HEAD.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nExtend 'ddx --version' (or add 'ddx doctor' check) to:\n1. Detect when invoked from inside a git repo whose origin matches the ddx project (DocumentDrivenDX/ddx).\n2. Compare the binary's build SHA (from -ldflags / main.go version injection) against the repo's HEAD.\n3. If HEAD is ahead of the binary's SHA, print a warning to stderr: 'WARNING: installed ddx is built from \u003cbinary-sha\u003e; source tree HEAD is \u003chead-sha\u003e (\u003cN\u003e commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.'\n4. Exit 0 (informational, not fatal).\n\nOptional: emit same warning on EVERY ddx command invocation when inside the ddx project (cheap: one git rev-parse).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Auto-rebuilding (operator should explicitly rebuild).\n- Comparing against upstream remote (only local source tree).\n- Detection from outside the ddx project repo.",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/cmd/version.go or cli/cmd/doctor.go detects when invoked from inside a git repo whose origin matches DocumentDrivenDX/ddx.\n2. When stale (HEAD ahead of build SHA), prints to stderr: 'WARNING: installed ddx is built from \u003cbinary-sha\u003e; source tree HEAD is \u003chead-sha\u003e (\u003cN\u003e commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.'\n3. Exit code 0 (informational only).\n4. TestVersion_StaleWarning_PrintsToStderr verifies warning appears when HEAD is ahead of injected SHA.\n5. TestVersion_NoWarningWhenInSync verifies no warning when HEAD matches build SHA.\n6. TestVersion_NoWarningOutsideDDxRepo verifies no warning when invoked from a non-ddx repo.\n7. TestVersion_HandlesDetachedHead verifies graceful handling of detached HEAD (warning OK or skipped).\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes."
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-89a9c305",
    "title": "server: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with unified spec in workers",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-04T02:20:25.071816485Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T16:13:02.852527831Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "area:server",
      "kind:refactor",
      "prevention",
      "triage:needs-investigation"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-29058e2a",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nWorker persistence and runtime execution still use a server-local ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec, so fields present at cobra or wire boundaries can be lost before spec.json or runWorker consumes them.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 defines ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec without MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, or SpecVersion. cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-845 builds CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime from that incomplete spec, so runWorker cannot honor those fields on server-spawned workers.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec, or make ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec a temporary type alias to executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec if needed for a mechanical migration.\n- Persist the fully resolved unified spec to .ddx/workers/\u003cid\u003e/spec.json after ApplyDefaults has already run.\n- Update lifecycleStartDetail, StartExecuteLoop, buildRoutePreflight, runWorker, and test literals to consume the unified spec.\n- Ensure runWorker passes MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev into config/loop/runtime paths, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD used only as the zero-value fallback.\n- Keep disk reads tolerant of unknown legacy fields such as min_tier and max_tier.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change queue picking, route preflight policy, landing behavior, or watchdog behavior.\n- Do not implement REST/GraphQL decode changes here except compile fixes.\n- Do not change LabelFilter semantics.",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/internal/server/workers.go no longer defines a server-local ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct; instead uses executeloop.ExecuteLoopSpec directly (or a confirmed type alias).\n2. Fully resolved spec (after ApplyDefaults) persisted to .ddx/workers/\u003cid\u003e/spec.json.\n3. lifecycleStartDetail, StartExecuteLoop, buildRoutePreflight, runWorker, and test literals updated to consume the unified spec.\n4. runWorker passes MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev into config/loop/runtime paths; escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD used only as zero-value fallback.\n5. Legacy disk reads tolerate unknown fields (min_tier, max_tier) without error.\n6. TestWorkers_ServerSpawnedWorker_HonorsMaxCostUSD verifies MaxCostUSD from spec reaches the executor.\n7. TestWorkers_UnifiedSpec_PersistsToSpecJson verifies spec.json contains all fields after ApplyDefaults.\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "issue_id": "ddx-89a9c305",
        "depends_on_id": "ddx-29058e2a",
        "type": "blocks",
        "created_at": "2026-05-04T02:20:34Z"
      }
    ]
  },

  {
    "id": "ddx-9228a484",
    "title": "C9: first-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation (absorbs ddx-cfedee8e + ddx-a8718bec + ddx-dc157075 behavior)",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T20:02:28.321106862Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T16:06:32.608909521Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "refactor",
      "kind:refactor"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-5cb6e6cd",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nExecute-loop cost-cap and stop logic is scattered across executor closure in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 1722-1730, 1818-1850) and ad-hoc loop continuation checks. Stopping conditions (Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked) are evaluated implicitly with no enum, making the stop logic brittle and hard to extend. The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) exists in cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go but has no production caller — dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must confirm before this child can safely wire or delete it.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- Cost cap: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722-1730 checks max_cost inside executor closure; cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818-1850 has a second cost-cap site.     "id": "ddx-9ca4b5bf",
    "title": "artifact-types: GraphQL Artifact.typeDefinitions resolver with size-capped on-demand file loading",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T07:06:56.346382288Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T16:20:24.986836493Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "story:17",
      "area:server",
      "kind:feature"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-43d67aa5",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nNo GraphQL resolver exists for Artifact.typeDefinitions. The ArtifactTypePanel (ddx-4fd71cf3) needs this resolver to show matched type definitions for an artifact. Without it, the frontend cannot retrieve template/prompt/example content for any artifact.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql (or equivalent): Artifact type does not have a typeDefinitions field.\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go: no typeDefinitions resolver function exists for the Artifact type.\n- The ddx library registers ArtifactTypeDefinitions under the library plugin structure, but no resolver maps from a library artifact path to its type definition prefix.\n- File content for template/prompt/example must be loaded on-demand (not at graph build time) with a size cap to avoid loading large files into memory.\n- ddx-1d52a30c (artifact type definition data model, a dep) must land first.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add typeDefinitions: [ArtifactTypeDefinition!]! field to the Artifact type in schema.graphql.\n- Add resolver in graphql_resolver.go that:\n  1. Takes artifact's prefix (via prefixOf helper).\n  2. Looks up all matching ArtifactTypeDefinition entries for that prefix (multi-match supported).\n  3. On-demand loads template/prompt/example file content with size cap (e.g., 64KB); truncates with a 'truncated' flag if exceeded.\n- Return array (not single) — prefix collision returns all matches.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Frontend rendering (ddx-4fd71cf3 handles that).\n- Caching (may be added later with ddx-4a7eed8c cache infrastructure).",
    "acceptance": "1. typeDefinitions: [ArtifactTypeDefinition!]! field exists on Artifact type in schema.graphql.\n2. Resolver returns all matching type definitions for the artifact's prefix (multi-match supported).\n3. File content loaded on-demand; content \u003e 64KB is truncated with isTruncated: true in the response.\n4. TestTypeDefinitions_SingleMatch returns one ArtifactTypeDefinition.\n5. TestTypeDefinitions_PrefixCollision_MultiMatch returns multiple ArtifactTypeDefinitions.\n6. TestTypeDefinitions_LargeFile_Truncated verifies file \u003e 64KB returns isTruncated=true.\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... green.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "issue_id": "ddx-9ca4b5bf",
        "depends_on_id": "ddx-1d52a30c",
        "type": "blocks",
        "created_at": "2026-05-02T07:06:56Z"
      }
    ]
  },

    "description": "PROBLEM\nThe execute-bead loop body (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036) has 18 store-error exit paths — every bead outcome write can hard-exit the drain loop. This violates the loop's contract: a single bead's store failure should never kill the entire drain worker.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:875-1036 contains ~14 direct error returns triggered by store write failures, all routing through handleOutcomeStoreError. Each returns a hard error to Drain instead of scheduling a cooldown and continuing. The helper handleOutcomeStoreError itself is also a terminal-exit path. The 18 exit paths (14 store-error sites + handleOutcomeStoreError variants) are the dominant smell: 18 of 24 returns in the loop body are store-error paths.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add cli/internal/agent/work/commit.go defining commitOutcome(ctx, store, beadID, op func() error).\n- commitOutcome calls op(); on error, calls SetExecutionCooldown with 'loop-error' ParkReason and returns nil (never hard-errors to Drain).\n- Delete handleOutcomeStoreError.\n- Replace all 14 callsites in execute_bead_loop.go (pre-refactor coordinates: 628, 740, 763, 800, 834, 850, 869, 877, 905, 918, 963, 979, 1014, 1026) with commitOutcome.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Logic changes to what's stored (only how store errors are handled).\n- execute_bead_loop.go file rename (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/internal/agent/work/commit.go (or cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go equivalent) defines commitOutcome(ctx, store, beadID string, op func() error) error.\n2. handleOutcomeStoreError deleted from execute_bead_loop.go.\n3. All 14 callsites in execute_bead_loop.go (pre-refactor: 628, 740, 763, 800, 834, 850, 869, 877, 905, 918, 963, 979, 1014, 1026) replaced with commitOutcome.\n4. TestCommitOutcome_StoreErrorContinuesLoop verifies that a store write failure schedules cooldown and returns nil (loop continues).\n5. TestCommitOutcome_StoreError_SchedulesCooldown_NotExit verifies cooldown event is recorded with ParkReason=loop-error.\n6. C0 fixture diff: store-glitch-during-close lifecycle byte-identical event stream (loop-error event present, no hard exit).\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

    "id": "ddx-b69f04f8",
    "title": "federation: spoke lifecycle — idempotent register, jittered heartbeat, URL-change handling",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T07:04:50.09115128Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T21:55:56.756629809Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "story:14",
      "area:server",
      "kind:feature"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-a038a090",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nWhen a spoke node starts with --hub-address, it must register with the hub and maintain a heartbeat so the hub's FederationRegistry knows the spoke is alive. Today no --hub-address flag exists and no spoke lifecycle (register / heartbeat / stale detection / URL-change re-register) is implemented.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/server/server.go: no --hub-address flag wired.\n- cli/internal/federation/: no spoke-side registration client or heartbeat loop exists.\n- The hub's FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012, a dep) provides the register endpoint and stale detection, but the spoke side has no code to call it.\n- Per locked decisions from the federation planning: register is idempotent on stable node_id; duplicate node_id rejected (not silently merged); heartbeat jittered at 30s; stale threshold at 2 minutes.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add --hub-address string flag to the server command (cli/cmd/server.go or equivalent).\n- Add cli/internal/federation/spoke_lifecycle.go:\n  - RegisterWithHub(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID, localURL): idempotent POST to hub register endpoint.\n  - StartHeartbeat(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID): jittered 30s heartbeat loop; on failure → stale detection.\n  - HandleURLChange(ctx, hubAddress, nodeID, newURL): re-register when local URL flips.\n- Stale threshold: hub marks spoke offline after 2 minutes without heartbeat.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Hub-side registry implementation (ddx-90f48012, a dep).\n- Federation query fan-out (existing fanout.go).\n- Mutation routing (S15-7b / ddx-eb75a32d).",
    "acceptance": "1. --hub-address flag wired to server command and passed to spoke lifecycle on startup.\n2. RegisterWithHub: idempotent (re-registration with same node_id does not duplicate); duplicate node_id (different node) hard-rejected with error.\n3. StartHeartbeat: heartbeat interval jittered around 30s.\n4. Hub marks spoke as offline after 2 minutes without heartbeat.\n5. HandleURLChange: re-registers when local URL differs from registered URL.\n6. TestSpokeLifecycle_Register_Idempotent verifies re-registration is a no-op.\n7. TestSpokeLifecycle_DuplicateNodeID_Rejected verifies distinct node with same ID is rejected.\n8. TestSpokeLifecycle_Heartbeat_30sJitter verifies heartbeat fires within [25s, 35s] window.\n9. TestSpokeLifecycle_URLChange_Triggers_Reregister verifies URL flip causes re-register.\n10. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.\n11. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

    "description": "PROBLEM\nIn the coordinator→client mutation path (Story 14/15), the originating peer identity must be forwarded from coordinator to spoke unchanged, and the spoke must authorize on both the origin AND the coordinator identity. Today cli/internal/federation/fanout.go supports only query fan-out with no identity forwarding; the mutation envelope from S15-7b carries headers but no production caller enforces dual authorization on the spoke side.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: query fan-out exists but no requireTrustedForwarded middleware or augmentation of requireTrusted handles forwarded mutations.\n- The spoke's requireTrusted (cli/internal/server/server.go) checks only the immediate transport peer — it does not parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity or X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers.\n- No dual-authorization path exists: a forwarded mutation that passes coordinator trust but carries an untrusted origin is not rejected.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add requireTrustedForwarded middleware (or augment requireTrusted) in cli/internal/server/server.go:\n  - Parse X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity from incoming request.\n  - Validate origin identity against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy.\n  - Validate coordinator identity against registered federation peers.\n  - Return 403 if either check fails or either header is missing.\n- Coordinator forwarding path (built in S15-7b): when forwarding a prompt-driven mutation, set X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified incoming peer identity (from WhoIs at coordinator edge); set X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Identity MUST NOT be rewritten in transit.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Identity signing/attestation structure (that's ddx-e2c217d1).\n- Mutation routing envelope (that's S15-7b / ddx-eb75a32d, a dep).\n- Non-mutation endpoints.",

TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_AuthorizesOriginAndCoordinator verifies both-trusted case returns 200.\n4. TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_OriginUntrusted returns 403.\n5. TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_CoordinatorUntrusted returns 403.\n6. TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_HeaderRewriteAttempt verifies header-not-matching-peer-attestation is rejected.\n7. Operators need occasional progress updates for each LLM turn, tool call, final response, context summary, and compaction event.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1680-1682 starts TailSessionLogs while ExecuteBead runs, and cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go:18-41 tails agent-*.jsonl files. cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go:80-250 formats existing session log event types (llm.request, llm.response, tool.call, compaction.end), but it has no parser/rendering path for the upstream fizeau progress event proposed in fizeau-acd91e9c and fizeau-0e13d680. service_run.go:188-194 drains fizeau service events into Result, but progress events are not retained or surfaced to stdout.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  - After fizeau-acd91e9c and fizeau-0e13d680 land, update DDx's fizeau dependency and add progress-event decoding in cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go.\n  - Render progress events as concise stdout lines: thinking start/update/complete with token and duration fields; tool start/complete with bounded command/name and duration; response start/complete with output tokens; context/compaction updates with bounded session summary.\n  - Keep output bounded and default-on for human output, but do not affect --json output.\n  - Ensure TailSessionLogs prints each progress line once and does not replay old session logs.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - Defining the upstream fizeau progress event schema; tracked in fizeau-acd91e9c.\n  - Subprocess harness progress synthesis in fizeau; tracked in fizeau-0e13d680.\n  - Changing execute-bead review or landing behavior.\n\nPARENT\n  No parent.\n\nDEPS\n  - fizeau-acd91e9c: upstream progress event schema/native service mapping.\n  - fizeau-0e13d680: upstream subprocess-backed harness progress parity.",
    "acceptance": "1. cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go recognizes fizeau progress event JSON and renders bounded human lines for thinking start/update/complete, tool start/complete, response completion, context summary, and compaction progress.\n2. TestFormatSessionLogLines_ProgressThinking verifies examples equivalent to \"thinking ...\" and \"thinking complete 8 tok in 5s\" render from progress events.\n3. TestFormatSessionLogLines_ProgressTool verifies examples equivalent to \"running tool call `ls -al` ...\" and \"completed in 3s, 100 tok\" render with bounded command text.\n4. TestFormatSessionLogLines_ProgressResponseAndContext verifies response token and session-context summary progress events render and remain bounded.\n5. TestTailSessionLogs_ProgressEventsNotReplayed verifies the tailer prints new progress lines once and does not replay pre-existing log lines.\n6. --json output from ddx work / ddx agent execute-loop remains structured JSON without human progress lines mixed in.\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes."

GraphQL routes through the same server-side helper as REST for ApplyDefaults, Validate, SpecVersion handling, ProjectRoot assignment, and StartExecuteLoop.\n5.     "description": "PROBLEM\nThe escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) was added in S10_2 but has no production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. Each bead currently gets exactly one singleTierAttempt without any rung escalation on substantive failure. The existing tier→profile mutation in the loop is an ad-hoc workaround that should be replaced by the ladder.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: singleTierAttempt is called exactly once per bead iteration; there is no loop over ladder rungs.\n- cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30: Ladder.Next is defined but has no call site in execute_bead_loop.go or any other production file (confirmed by dead-code audit ddx-09d2990c).\n- The existing tier→profile mutation (a manual MinPower bump) in the executor closure is the only escalation behavior; it is not driven by the ladder contract.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Replace the single singleTierAttempt call in execute_bead_loop.go with a real loop that:\n  1. Calls singleTierAttempt with current rung's MinPower.\n  2. On substantive failure (non-park, non-budget-exceeded), calls ladder.Next to get next rung.\n  3. Terminates when ladder.Next returns no further rungs or a stop condition triggers.\n- Remove the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder.Next drives all MinPower escalation.\n- Pass Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='\u003cbead_id\u003e:\u003cattempt_id\u003e' on every ExecuteRequest.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Changing the ladder definition or rung configuration (S10_2 owns that).\n- Cost-cap relocation (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484).",

TestEscalationLadder_WiredIntoExecutor verifies ladder.Next is called when singleTierAttempt returns a substantive failure.\n5. TestEscalationLadder_StopsAtFinalRung verifies loop terminates when ladder.Next exhausts rungs.\n6.     "id": "ddx-e2c217d1",
    "title": "operator-prompts: multi-node trust attestation forwarding (gates non-localhost write)",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T07:05:44.369592605Z",

      "phase:2",
      "story:15",
      "area:server",
      "kind:feature",
      "blocks-remote"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-1d85c927",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nBLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement in coordinator topology. When the hub forwards a mutation to a spoke, there is no signed attestation of origin identity, and the spoke cannot distinguish a legitimate coordinator-forwarded request from a hub-originated forgery. Without dual authorization on both origin and coordinator identity, any compromised hub can submit mutations on behalf of any operator.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: no attestation metadata structure defined; no signing of identity claims.\n- cli/internal/server/server.go: requireTrusted checks only immediate transport peer (hub); does not validate origin identity independently.\n- wake-on-approve routing (the 'wake-on-approve targets owning client node's loop' requirement) has no implementation path in the current federation layer.\n- ddx-f171a715 (federation foundation, a dep) and ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Define attestation metadata structure: signed structured metadata carrying origin identity (WhoIs peer at coordinator edge) and coordinator identity (hub's own attested ID). Signing mechanism: HMAC or similar per plan-locked decisions.\n- Spoke validates BOTH: origin identity (verified against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy) AND hub identity (verified against registered federation peers).\n- wake-on-approve: when spoke receives an approve mutation, it routes wake signal to the owning client node's execute-loop (project-ownership authoritative on the spoke).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Transport-level encryption (handled by tailnet).\n- Key management or certificate infrastructure beyond HMAC shared secret.\n- Story 14 query fan-out behavior.",
    "acceptance": "1. Attestation metadata structure defined (signed) in cli/internal/federation/ or cli/internal/server/.\n2. Spoke validates both origin AND hub identity on forwarded mutations; returns 403 when either fails.\n3. wake-on-approve mutation routed to owning spoke's execute-loop.\n4. TestAttestation_ValidOriginAndHub_Accepts verifies happy path.\n5. TestAttestation_SpoofedOrigin_Rejects verifies spoofed origin identity returns 403.\n6. TestAttestation_UntrustedHub_Rejects verifies untrusted hub returns 403.\n7. TestWakeOnApprove_RoutesToOwningSpoke verifies wake signal reaches correct spoke.\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

    "status": "open",
    "priority": 1,
    "issue_type": "task",

    "description": "PROBLEM\nFederation today supports query fan-out only (cli/internal/federation/fanout.go). For the multi-node operator-prompt path, the coordinator (hub) must route a mutation to a single spoke that owns the project, NOT fan out. No mutation routing envelope, no project-based spoke selection, and no forwarding client exist.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: FanOut function sends read queries to all spokes; has no concept of routing to a single owning spoke for mutations.\n- FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012): stores spoke records by node_id but no lookup-by-project-id helper exists.\n- No HTTP client method ForwardMutation exists in the federation package.\n- Plan-locked decision (point 6 from story-15 planning): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations; mutation envelope is HTTP POST to spoke /graphql with X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers preserved verbatim.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry FederationRegistry, projectID string) (*SpokeRecord, error): looks up the spoke that owns the project; returns error when no owner registered.\n- Add federation client method ForwardMutation(ctx context.Context, target *SpokeRecord, body []byte, headers map[string]string) (*http.Response, error): issues HTTP POST to \u003cspoke\u003e/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.\n- Add hub HTTP handler that accepts forwarding requests and routes by project (the handler that S15-7c and S15-7d build on top of).\n- Identity header values are NOT populated in this bead (that is S15-7c / ddx-ba31fec8); this bead lays the envelope and routing.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Dual authorization on the spoke side (ddx-ba31fec8).\n- Identity attestation structure (ddx-e2c217d1).\n- Changing existing query fan-out behavior.",
    "acceptance": "1. RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry, projectID) returns correct SpokeRecord or error when no owner registered.\n2. Hub handler that accepts mutation forwarding requests exists and routes by project.\n4. TestRouteMutation_CorrectSpoke verifies routing to the correct spoke.\n5. TestRouteMutation_NoOwner_ReturnsError verifies error when project owner unknown.\n6. TestForwardMutation_HeadersReachSpoke verifies headers arrive at spoke unchanged.\n7.     "id": "ddx-f1e12904",
    "title": "review: cost cap + reviewer routing/downgrade + refusal contracts",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 2,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T07:06:56.655171593Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-04T16:25:43.34277059Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "story:18",
      "area:server",
      "kind:feature"
    ],
    "parent": "ddx-42b917fe",
    "description": "PROBLEM\nReview sessions have no cost cap enforcement, no reviewer routing policy, and no structured refusal contracts. Without these, a review session can run unbounded cost, use an unavailable reviewer, or fail with an unstructured error that the frontend cannot surface to the operator.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/server/review_session.go (from ddx-e7b80a50, a dep): ReviewSession has max_billable_usd field but no enforcement code.\n- No reviewer routing logic exists in the server review path — preferred_reviewer from Story 17 and the escalation ladder from Story 10 are not consulted.\n- No structured refusal error codes exist (PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE).\n- ddx-0e5c3005 (reviewer routing contract, a dep) must land first.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Enforce max_billable_usd per session: before each turn, check cumulative cost_usd against max_billable_usd; if exceeded, return COST_CAP_EXCEEDED refusal.\n- Reviewer routing: consult Story 17's preferred_reviewer first; fall back to Story 10's escalation ladder.\n- Downgrade routing: when preferred reviewer is unavailable, downgrade to next ladder rung and emit review-pairing-degraded event.\n- Implement three structured refusal error codes as typed errors with structured JSON body.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Turn dispatcher implementation (ddx-f7c3d512, a dep).\n- Frontend display of refusal codes (handled by review panel bead).",
    "acceptance": "1. max_billable_usd enforced per session: turn rejected with COST_CAP_EXCEEDED when cumulative cost_usd would exceed limit.\n2. Reviewer routing consults Story 17 preferred_reviewer first, then Story 10 escalation ladder.\n3. Downgrade routing: unavailable preferred reviewer falls back to next ladder rung with review-pairing-degraded event.\n4. Three refusal codes implemented: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED, COST_CAP_EXCEEDED, REVIEWER_UNAVAILABLE.\n5. TestReview_CostCap_ReturnsCostCapExceeded verifies enforcement at max_billable_usd.\n6. TestReview_ReviewerUnavailable_Downgrades verifies downgrade path and event.\n7. TestReview_RefusalCodes_HaveStructuredBody verifies all three codes return JSON body.\n8. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... green.\n9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",
    "dependencies": [
      {
        "issue_id": "ddx-f1e12904",
        "depends_on_id": "ddx-0e5c3005",
        "type": "blocks",
        "created_at": "2026-05-02T07:06:56Z"
      }
    ]

The same anti-pattern likely exists in other ddx commands where flags travel cobra → function param → loose map → HTTP request struct → spec struct → consumer. Find them and either:\n- File unification beads following the ExecuteLoopSpec pattern, OR\n- Document them as already-clean (and why).\n\nCOMMANDS TO AUDIT (per codex review — expanded)\n\n1. ddx agent run (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go ~395-414 cobra flags) PLUS its server endpoint /api/agent/run (cli/internal/server/server.go:2150-2188) AND its MCP equivalent (cli/internal/server/server.go:4537-4553). The server endpoint accepts only Harness/Prompt/Model/Effort while cobra has many more flags — same drop risk as ExecuteLoop, narrower fields.\n2. ddx work passthrough (alias for ddx agent execute-loop with implicit OpaquePassthrough — covered by ddx-29058e2a but verify the implicit behavior is documented in the spec)\n3. Record as N/A; do not chase.\n8. ddx run — same: if not shipped, N/A.\n\nAUDIT METHOD (per codex — generated diff, not gut-feel)\n\nFor each command:\n1. Enumerate cobra flag list (programmatic: parse cobra command struct or grep for `cmd.Flags().*Var`).\n2. Enumerate corresponding server JSON request struct fields (programmatic: AST parse the request struct in handler).\n3. Enumerate the spec/persisted struct fields (programmatic).\n4. Enumerate the final consumer fields (programmatic).\n5. Diff each layer's field set; classify each field as: execution / control-plane / output-only / unsupported / dropped.\n6. Drop-risk score: count of fields that exist in cobra but NOT in subsequent layers.\n\nOUTPUT\n\nReport at .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/parallel-struct-audit.md:\n- One section per command audited\n- For each: layer count, list of fields per layer, classification table, drop-risk score, recommendation (unify / clean / N/A)\n- Summary table: command | layer count | drop-risk count | recommendation\n\nIf unification candidates found, file follow-up beads using the ExecuteLoopSpec refactor (ddx-29058e2a) as the template.\n\nDEP CHANGE (per codex)\n- DEP on ddx-29058e2a is RELAXED to \"may begin read-only command discovery + diff generation in parallel; only the implementation-pattern recommendations need ddx-29058e2a as reference.\" Update dep semantics so this can start now.",

For each command: programmatically-generated field tables for each layer (cobra / REST request struct / GraphQL adapter / spec / consumer); diff between layers; explicit drop count per command.\n3. Drop-risk score (count of fields dropped between any two layers) explicit per command.\n4. Summary table at top of report.\n5. data.text: The bead is definitely oversized, but it isn’t the same shape as the earlier examples: two prerequisite/dependency beads are already closed, and I still need to see whether there are existing child beads for this exact parent. If there are, the right outcome is a no-changes rationale, not a fresh decomposition.

  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:940-1090 contains the outcome adjudication and cooldown branch chain. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:976 and 1084 call shouldSuppressNoProgress before applying no-progress cooldown. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1099 appends the report to the loop result after cooldown handling. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:30 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook and cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go defines TriageResult, but no cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go exists and the loop never calls the hook.

If package boundaries require a different name, preserve the explicit injected inputs.

    "description": "PROBLEM
  ddx try needs lint and triage extension points plus an outcome classification carrier before hook implementations can be wired safely. Today ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime exposes PreClaimHook and RoutePreflight only, and ExecuteBeadReport carries Disrupted/DisruptionReason but no sibling OutcomeReason for post-attempt triage.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:24-38 defines ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with PreClaimHook and RoutePreflight closures but no PreDispatchLintHook or PostAttemptTriageHook slot. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:198-266 defines ExecuteBeadReport with Disrupted and DisruptionReason but no OutcomeReason field. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1522-1532 suppresses no-progress cooldowns only through Disrupted/BaseRev/ResultRev checks.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Add LintResult, TriageResult, and FollowupBead structs in cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go or alongside ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.
  - Add ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreDispatchLintHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error).
  - Add ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error).
  - Add ExecuteBeadReport.OutcomeReason string beside Disrupted/DisruptionReason, preserving the existing Disrupted bool and JSON shape.
  - Include outcome_reason in executeBeadLoopEvent body serialization when populated.
  - Extend shouldSuppressNoProgress so OutcomeReason values transport, quota, routing, timeout, and merge_conflict also bypass no-progress cooldown; keep the existing Disrupted short-circuit unchanged.

NON-SCOPE
  - Invoking either hook in the loop; covered by sibling child beads.
  - Runner-backed bead-lifecycle skill calls; covered by lint/triage implementation child beads.
  - cli/cmd/try.go wiring; covered by the CLI wiring child bead.
  - GraphQL exposure of OutcomeReason.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  ddx-e1a576a7 per decomposition edge requested by the parent attempt instructions.",

2. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime exposes PreDispatchLintHook and PostAttemptTriageHook with signatures func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error) and func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error).
3. ExecuteBeadReport includes OutcomeReason string with json tag outcome_reason,omitempty beside the existing Disrupted and DisruptionReason fields; Disrupted remains unchanged.
4. TestReport_OutcomeReason_Persists_BesideDisrupted verifies executeBeadLoopEvent records outcome_reason while preserving disrupted/disruption_reason behavior.
5. TestSuppressNoProgress_HonorsTransientReasons verifies OutcomeReason values transport, quota, routing, timeout, and merge_conflict bypass no-progress cooldown while non-transient reasons retain existing BaseRev/ResultRev behavior.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes."

    "parent": "ddx-e1a576a7",
    "description": "PROBLEM
  ddx try must invoke bead-quality lint after selecting a candidate and before claiming it, but the execute-bead loop currently has no lint hook invocation or warn/block policy at that point.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:640-714 runs PreClaimHook and RoutePreflight before Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:668-710 is the current pre-claim RoutePreflight gate; cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:714 calls Store.Claim. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:29 now exposes ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreDispatchLintHook from closed contract bead ddx-c8dc1146, but no loop path invokes it and no path emits bead-quality.lint events.

PROPOSED FIX
  - In cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go, invoke runtime.PreDispatchLintHook(ctx, candidate.ID) after RoutePreflight succeeds and before Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee).
  - Default behavior is WARN-ONLY: append a bead event kind bead-quality.lint containing score, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied, and any warning details, then continue to claim regardless of score.
  - Add opt-in BLOCK behavior from resolved config key bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score. Only a valid hook response with Score below threshold refuses dispatch; infrastructure errors fail open.
  - On block, emit operator-readable log/loop event text citing bead-lifecycle SKILL.md guidance and skip claiming that bead. The skipped bead must be marked attempted for this Run so the loop moves on instead of spinning on the same candidate.
  - Treat hook errors, missing skill/harness, bad JSON surfaced through the hook, and timeouts as warning events and proceed with claim.

NON-SCOPE
  - Defining hook/result types; closed in ddx-c8dc1146.
  - Implementing the runner-backed lint hook body; covered by ddx-68706ef9.
  - Post-attempt triage and OutcomeReason handling; covered by ddx-4375292b.
  - ddx work integration.

PARENT
  ddx-e1a576a7.

DEPS
  No deps. ddx-c8dc1146 is closed and its hook contract is available in cli/internal/agent/quality_hooks.go and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.",
    "acceptance": "1. PreDispatchLintHook fires after RoutePreflight success and before Store.Claim in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go; if RoutePreflight rejects, lint is not invoked for that candidate.
2. TestLoop_LintHook_FiresPreClaim proves hook execution happens before claim by observing hook/claim ordering.
3. TestLintHook_LowScore_WarnDoesNotBlock proves a low score appends a bead-quality.lint event and the loop still claims/dispatches by default.
4. TestLintHook_BlockMode_RefusesDispatchOnLowScore proves bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score blocks only when the hook returns a valid score below threshold, with no Store.Claim call for that bead.
5. TestLintHook_SkillMissing_ProceedsWithWarning, TestLintHook_BadJSON_ProceedsWithWarning, and TestLintHook_Timeout_ProceedsWithWarning prove hook infrastructure failures append warnings and proceed with claim.
6. WIRED-IN: git grep PreDispatchLintHook cli/internal/agent shows the execute-loop invocation site, not just the runtime field declaration.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes."
  }

ID:       ddx-a9d130d0
Title:    EPIC: bead-lifecycle quality framework — make P7 mechanically true (lint + triage)
Type:     epic
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, area:beads, area:agent, kind:platform, reliability, bead-quality, adr:023
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-e34994e2
Desc:     PROBLEM
Per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 P7 ('bead = sufficient sub-agent prompt'), bead authoring quality is a dominant factor in execution success. Audit-1 (commit 86848d21) covered 20 beads; audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) covered 108 beads; 88 of 108 scored ≤5 against the rubric. Evidence bead ddx-f339c399 documents one observed no_changes outcome (ddx-29058e2a, 2026-05-04) traced to bead-quality gap.

ROOT CAUSE
Today nothing mechanically prevents authoring a bead that's insufficient as a sub-agent prompt OR catches a non-clean execution outcome before it triggers cooldown. The 9 fail-prone layers in ddx try / ddx work (per RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES) are mostly fail-closed; bead authoring quality has no observability or enforcement layer at all.

PROPOSED FRAMEWORK
A SKILL-based bead-lifecycle quality framework, layered onto the existing ddx skill (matching the bead-breakdown/ sub-skill pattern at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md). Three modes within ONE sub-skill: lint (pre-dispatch), triage (post-attempt outcome classification), refine (suggest amendments). ddx try integrates via small Go hooks that compose the existing layer-1 runner library to invoke the skill.

NO new top-level skills. NO new ddx CLI verbs. NO LLM judgment baked into Go code.

CHILD BEADS (4 sequential phases)
1. ADR-023 + FEAT amendments (governance + policy)
2. Sub-skill ships to all 5 paths + curated examples
3. ddx try hook integration (warn-only mode) + OutcomeReason field
4. Migration: close stale dups + retrofit 40 HIGH beads (during warn-only; collects baseline)

After child #4 confirms baseline acceptable false-positive rate, BLOCK mode is enabled via config (no separate bead).

NON-SCOPE
- ddx work plan quality column (defer; informational, not blocking)
- ddx bead refine standalone command (defer; refine mode in skill is invocable via ddx run)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first; package later per FEAT-015 project-local model)
- in_progress-as-eligible bug (separate concern; queue correctness, not bead quality)

GOVERNANCE
- ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md (codex correction: not 03-decide/)
- FEAT-004 amend: bead validation hooks + waiver storage + tracker metadata (FEAT-004:150 already owns bead validation hooks)
- FEAT-010 amend: ddx try / ddx work hook ordering + retry classification + outcome records
- FEAT-011 amend: nested workflow-skill packaging (FEAT-011:180 stale — says DDx ships exactly one skill, but bead-breakdown/ exists)
- FEAT-001 amend ONLY if --force --reason CLI flag added (defer to child #3 if needed)
Accept:   1. All 4 child beads closed.
2. ADR-023 status = Accepted.
3. FEAT-004, FEAT-010, FEAT-011 amendments committed.
4. Sub-skill .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md exists in all 5 shipped paths (codex: skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).
5. ddx try hooks fire in WARN-ONLY mode by default; BLOCK mode opt-in via config.
6. Baseline lint score distribution recorded post-migration; threshold for BLOCK mode chosen empirically.
7. Closed via final operator review confirming framework is operational.
